Two diametrically opposite points $P, Q$ lie on an infinitely long cylinder
which has radius $2/\pi$. A piece of string with length 8 has its ends joined
to $P$, is wrapped once round the outside of the cylinder, and then has its
midpoint joined to $Q$ (so there is length 4 of the string on each side of the
cylinder). A paint brush is attached to the string so that it can slide along
the full length the string. Find the area of the outside surface of the
cylinder which can be painted by the brush.
